evals.prompt: Set up my product context. I'm in my project repo — it's a Next.js app called Fenceline, a neighbor-dispute documentation tool. The landing page copy and pricing page are in the repo. Ask me whatever you can't figure out yourself.
evals.expected_output: The agent inspects repo files first, drafts inferable fields (name, description, pricing, stack) from them, asks one batched set of questions only for unknowable fields (stage, 30-day goal, objections, channels), then writes a filled product-context.md to the project root.
assertions: The agent inspects the repo for landing/pricing copy before asking questions
Questions are batched rather than asked one at a time
Questions target only fields not inferable from the repo
A product-context.md file is written to the project root using the template structure
The output summarizes what was inferred vs. asked vs. left empty

evals.prompt: update my product-context.md — we finally launched last week and switched from the waitlist to $29/mo with a 7-day trial.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads the existing product-context.md, updates the stage (waitlist → live) and pricing fields, checks whether related fields are now stale (launch goals, channels), and preserves all other content including custom sections.
assertions: The existing file is read before editing
Stage and pricing fields are updated to the new values
Other fields and any custom sections are preserved, not regenerated
The agent flags related fields that may now be stale (e.g. 30-day goal) rather than silently leaving them
